key_insight: TREK expands a model’s reasoning support with teacher-generated verified solutions before refining on-policy, helping GRPO escape hard prompts whose correct modes are initially out of reach.
engineering_relevance: For training strong reasoning agents, TREK offers a practical two-stage recipe that can exploit black-box, white-box, or self-context teachers without needing teacher internals.
method: Find prompts where the student’s pass rate is very low.
Generate verified candidate solutions from a proposal source and keep the top-r proposals by student likelihood.
Apply a short forward-KL distillation phase to add these modes to support, then resume standard GRPO refinement.
evidence_signal: In the abstract, TREK improves Qwen3 models on AIME 2024/2025 (e.g., Qwen3-8B rises from 36.9 to 40.3 on AIME 2025 and from 47.9 to 51.1 on AIME 2024), and also boosts ALFWorld and ScienceWorld success rates.
watch_item: TREK depends on access to verified candidate trajectories, so gains may be limited by the quality/cost of the proposal source and by the ability to reliably verify outputs.
paper_title: TREK: Distill to Explore, Reinforce to Refine
